description.en: Basic interface for resolving entities. If you create an object implementing this interface, then register the object with your Parser, the parser will call the method in your object to resolve all external entities.

description.en: Interface used by the parser to present error and warning messages to the application. The methods of this object control whether errors are immediately converted to exceptions or are handled in some other way.

description.en: Reports the end of a CDATA marked section.

description.en: Reports the end of DTD declaration.

excerpt.en: Reports the end of DTD declaration.

description.en: Receive notification of the end of a document. The SAX parser will invoke this method only once, and it will be the last method invoked during the parse. The parser shall not invoke this method until it has either abandoned parsing (because of an unrecoverable error) or reached the end of input.

description.en: Signals the end of an element in non-namespace mode. The name parameter contains the name of the element type, just as with the startElement() event.

excerpt.es: Signals the end of an element in non-namespace mode. The name parameter contains the name of the element type, just as with the startElement() event.
excerpt.en: Signals the end of an element in non-namespace mode. The name parameter contains the name of the element type, just as with the startElement() event.

description.en: Signals the end of an element in namespace mode. The name parameter contains the name of the element type, just as with the startElementNS() method, likewise the qname parameter.

excerpt.es: Signals the end of an element in namespace mode. The name parameter contains the name of the element type, just as with the startElementNS() method, likewise the qname parameter.
excerpt.en: Signals the end of an element in namespace mode. The name parameter contains the name of the element type, just as with the startElementNS() method, likewise the qname parameter.

description.en: End the scope of a prefix-URI mapping. See startPrefixMapping() for details. This event will always occur after the corresponding endElement() event, but the order of endPrefixMapping() events is not otherwise guaranteed.

description.en: Called when the parser encounters a recoverable error. If this method does not raise an exception, parsing may continue, but further document information should not be expected by the application. Allowing the parser to continue may allow additional errors to be discovered in the input document.
